forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS36PZK9K6PHTRB9M7YPWTF2
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8680'
+project: command-service
+title: 'SDK convenience: `this.commands.command.command.*` registration helpers'
+---
+## What
+
+Add a thin SDK helper in the plugin platform's TypeScript runtime so plugins don't have to spell out `this.commands.command({ op: "register command", id: ..., name: ..., available: ..., execute: ... })` for every command. The `_meta` operations tree gives us `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` path sugar for free (see plugin-architecture.md *Service Consumption*) ŌĆö this task verifies that path sugar works for the Command service and adds an optional `registerMany(commands[])` batch helper.
+
+Files (in `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/` ŌĆö virtual `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` module served to plugins):
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts` ŌĆö `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])` helper that loops over an array of `CommandRegistration` objects, calling `plugin.commands.command.command.register(...)` for each. Returns disposables that `plugin.track()` already auto-handles. Optional, plugins can also call the verb directly.
+
+This is a tiny ergonomics layer ŌĆö the whole point of the built-in plugins being TypeScript is that they don't need a special API. But a 17-command perspective plugin shouldn't repeat the same boilerplate 17 times.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.register({ id, name, execute, ... })` works in a TS plugin (path sugar from `_meta`)
+- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.list({})` returns active commands
+- [ ] `registerCommands(this, commands[])` helper exists in `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` and loops register calls
+- [ ] Auto-cleanup still works ŌĆö the ledger captures each registration
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/command_sdk_e2e.rs` ŌĆö probe plugin uses both forms (direct + batch helper); both result in `list` returning the registered commands; unload purges them
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test integration` passes
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the e2e test first using both forms; verify both produce the same observable state.
+
+Small task; depends on the Command service being live in the host (previous task).
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,31 +3,51 @@
 - claude-code
 depends_on:
 - 01KS36PZK9K6PHTRB9M7YPWTF2
+- 01KS371KNY4YARZ67KWVSXPDFP
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8680'
 project: command-service
-title: 'SDK convenience: `this.commands.command.command.*` registration helpers'
+title: 'SDK helpers: `ensureServices` + `registerCommands` (plugin convention)'
 ---
 ## What

-Add a thin SDK helper in the plugin platform's TypeScript runtime so plugins don't have to spell out `this.commands.command({ op: "register command", id: ..., name: ..., available: ..., execute: ... })` for every command. The `_meta` operations tree gives us `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` path sugar for free (see plugin-architecture.md *Service Consumption*) ŌĆö this task verifies that path sugar works for the Command service and adds an optional `registerMany(commands[])` batch helper.
+Add two SDK helpers in the plugin platform's TypeScript runtime that codify the convention every command-registering plugin follows:
+
+1. `ensureServices(plugin, serviceNames[])` ŌĆö registers each named service from the host's exposed-modules table, idempotently. Relies on the plugin-arch idempotency work: if another plugin already registered the same `(name, source)`, this call is a no-op.
+2. `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])` ŌĆö loops over an array of `CommandRegistration` objects calling `plugin.commands.command.command.register(...)` for each. Returns disposables (auto-tracked by the platform ledger).
+
+The convention: a plugin's `load()` first calls `ensureServices(this, ["commands"])` (plus `"window"`, `"app"` if needed), THEN calls `registerCommands(this, [...])`. This makes plugins self-contained ŌĆö they declare which services they depend on rather than assuming the host pre-registered them.
+
+```ts
+// Convention for any command-registering plugin's load():
+async load() {
+  await ensureServices(this, ["commands", "window"]);
+  await registerCommands(this, [
+    { id: "task.move", name: "Move Task", /* ... */, execute: async (ctx) => { ... } },
+    /* ... */
+  ]);
+}
+```

 Files (in `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/` ŌĆö virtual `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` module served to plugins):
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts` ŌĆö `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])` helper that loops over an array of `CommandRegistration` objects, calling `plugin.commands.command.command.register(...)` for each. Returns disposables that `plugin.track()` already auto-handles. Optional, plugins can also call the verb directly.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/services.ts` ŌĆö `ensureServices(plugin, names[])`. Calls `plugin.register(name, { rust: <host-side id> })` for each. Looks up the rust-side id from a small lookup table provided by the host (`commands` ŌåÆ `command_service`, `window` ŌåÆ `window_service`, `app` ŌåÆ `app_service`, etc.). The lookup table is part of `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` and updated as services are added.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts` ŌĆö `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])`

-This is a tiny ergonomics layer ŌĆö the whole point of the built-in plugins being TypeScript is that they don't need a special API. But a 17-command perspective plugin shouldn't repeat the same boilerplate 17 times.
+Why `ensureServices` exists rather than letting plugins call `this.register("commands", { rust: "command_service" })` directly: the rust-side id (`command_service` vs `commands` the public name) is a host-internal detail; the helper lets the host evolve the underlying module name without breaking plugins. Plugins just say "I need the commands service."

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.register({ id, name, execute, ... })` works in a TS plugin (path sugar from `_meta`)
-- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.list({})` returns active commands
-- [ ] `registerCommands(this, commands[])` helper exists in `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` and loops register calls
-- [ ] Auto-cleanup still works ŌĆö the ledger captures each registration
+- [ ] `ensureServices(this, ["commands"])` registers the `commands` server if not yet registered; no-op if already registered with the same source
+- [ ] After `ensureServices`, `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` works (path sugar from `_meta`)
+- [ ] `registerCommands(this, [...])` loops register calls; auto-cleanup via the ledger
+- [ ] Calling `ensureServices` from two different plugins in the same host works ŌĆö second is idempotent no-op
+- [ ] If a different plugin registered `commands` with a DIFFERENT source, `ensureServices` returns the `ServerNameTaken` error (per the plugin-arch idempotency task's contract)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/command_sdk_e2e.rs` ŌĆö probe plugin uses both forms (direct + batch helper); both result in `list` returning the registered commands; unload purges them
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/ensure_services_e2e.rs` ŌĆö two probe plugins both call `ensureServices(this, ["commands"])` in `load()`; both succeed; both register a unique command; `list` returns both; unload one ŌĆö its commands purged but `commands` server remains live for the other
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/command_sdk_e2e.rs` ŌĆö probe plugin uses `ensureServices` + `registerCommands` (the convention) and the direct `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` form; both produce the same observable state; unload purges both
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test integration` passes

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the e2e test first using both forms; verify both produce the same observable state.
+- Use `/tdd` ŌĆö write the two-plugin shared-service test first; it pins the convention.

-Small task; depends on the Command service being live in the host (previous task).
\ No newline at end of file
+Depends on the Command service being live in the host (previous task) AND on plugin-arch's `ServerRegistry::register` idempotency task.
\ No newline at end of file

+Add a thin SDK helper in the plugin platform's TypeScript runtime so plugins don't have to spell out `this.commands.command({ op: "register command", id: ..., name: ..., available: ..., execute: ... })` for every command. The `_meta` operations tree gives us `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` path sugar for free (see plugin-architecture.md *Service Consumption*) ŌĆö this task verifies that path sugar works for the Command service and adds an optional `registerMany(commands[])` batch helper.

 Files (in `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/` ŌĆö virtual `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` module served to plugins):

Looks up the rust-side id from a small lookup table provided by the host (`commands` ŌåÆ `command_service`, `window` ŌåÆ `window_service`, `app` ŌåÆ `app_service`, etc.). The lookup table is part of `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` and updated as services are added.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts` ŌĆö `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])`
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/src/sdk/commands.ts` ŌĆö `registerCommands(plugin, commands[])` helper that loops over an array of `CommandRegistration` objects, calling `plugin.commands.command.command.register(...)` for each. Returns disposables that `plugin.track()` already auto-handles. Optional, plugins can also call the verb directly.

-Why `ensureServices` exists rather than letting plugins call `this.register("commands", { rust: "command_service" })` directly: the rust-side id (`command_service` vs `commands` the public name) is a host-internal detail; the helper lets the host evolve the underlying module name without breaking plugins. Plugins just say "I need the commands service."
+This is a tiny ergonomics layer ŌĆö the whole point of the built-in plugins being TypeScript is that they don't need a special API. But a 17-command perspective plugin shouldn't repeat the same boilerplate 17 times.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `ensureServices(this, ["commands"])` registers the `commands` server if not yet registered; no-op if already registered with the same source
-- [ ] After `ensureServices`, `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` works (path sugar from `_meta`)
-- [ ] `registerCommands(this, [...])` loops register calls; auto-cleanup via the ledger
-- [ ] Calling `ensureServices` from two different plugins in the same host works ŌĆö second is idempotent no-op
-- [ ] If a different plugin registered `commands` with a DIFFERENT source, `ensureServices` returns the `ServerNameTaken` error (per the plugin-arch idempotency task's contract)
+- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.register({ id, name, execute, ... })` works in a TS plugin (path sugar from `_meta`)
+- [ ] `await this.commands.command.command.list({})` returns active commands
+- [ ] `registerCommands(this, commands[])` helper exists in `@swissarmyhammer/plugin` and loops register calls

-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/command_sdk_e2e.rs` ŌĆö probe plugin uses `ensureServices` + `registerCommands` (the convention) and the direct `this.commands.command.command.register(...)` form; both produce the same observable state; unload purges both
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-plugin/tests/integration/command_sdk_e2e.rs` ŌĆö probe plugin uses both forms (direct + batch helper); both result in `list` returning the registered commands; unload purges them
 - [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-plugin --test integration` passes
